commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

commit.message:
Fix crawler pipeline: remove broken partition filter and fix Flyway version

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Cicada, Expressway, Gavel writeups; rewrite DarkZero from actual walkthrough

- Rewrote htb-darkzero.md to match real attack chain (Rubeus, PetitPotam, Golden Ticket)

- Added htb-expressway.md (easy/Linux — IKE PSK crack, CVE-2025-32463)
- Added htb-gavel.md (medium/Linux — git-dumper, SQLi, PHP rule engine RCE, SUID bash)
- Copied all writeup images to public/images/writeups/{cicada,expressway,gavel}/

fix(build): remove JSX.Element return types to fix Vercel TS build

JSX namespace isn't available without explicit React import in this
tsconfig setup. Dropping the redundant explicit return types lets TS
infer them, unblocking the Vercel production build.

commit.message:
fix(build): exclude test files from tsc -b and restore JSX types

- Add src/vite-env.d.ts (standard Vite pattern) so vite/client types
  are available without overriding TypeScript's "types" field
- Remove "types": ["vite/client"] from tsconfig.app.json — this was
  blocking @types/react auto-discovery, causing JSX.Element errors
- Exclude *.test.ts/tsx from tsconfig.app.json so production build
  does not compile Vitest globals (vi, describe, expect)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: receipt_manager
repository.description: Mobile Receipt Manager to share costs with others

commit.message:
feat(security): move Anthropic API key server-side via Vercel function

api/parse-receipt.ts — new Vercel serverless function: receives base64
images, calls Anthropic with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (server-side secret),
returns ParseReceiptResult JSON.

src/ai/parseReceipt.ts — remove @anthropic-ai/sdk import, POST to
/api/parse-receipt instead. API key never reaches the browser bundle.

Tests updated to mock fetch instead of @anthropic-ai/sdk — all 9 pass.

fix: add SSE error recovery and permit /error in JWT filter and security config

- Wrap chat and generatePlan endpoints in Flux.defer + onErrorResume to emit error SSE events instead of propagating exceptions
- Add onErrorResume in StreamingService to gracefully terminate SSE streams on error
- Permit DispatcherType.ERROR in AgentSecurityConfig to prevent Tomcat ERROR dispatch from corrupting SSE/HTTP2 frames
- Bypass JWT filter for /error path in JwtValidationFilter
- Add OSM_USE_DUMMY=true to ai-agent-service in docker-compose.yml

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(dockerhost): use --force-recreate on boot to fix stale network namespaces

Docker's restart policy can start containers before compose-up runs,
leaving them with broken Gluetun network bindings despite appearing
"running". Force-recreate on first attempt ensures clean namespaces.

commit.message:
Add Agents section to CLAUDE.md and gitignore status.md

Promote agent usage and atomic commit/PR guidelines into dedicated
sections in CLAUDE.md. Add status.md to .gitignore for local-only
project status tracking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: robust rate limit handling + newsletter email system

Sync rate limit fix:
- Pre-flight rate limit check via /rate_limit API before starting
- Smart waiting: when rate limited, WAIT for actual reset time (~60min)
  instead of giving up after 2 retries
- Inter-phase rate checks: verify budget between phases, wait if needed
- Enrichment budget cap: max 500 API calls per sync for owner data
- Network error retry with exponential backoff
- GitHub Actions timeout increased to 120 minutes
- Removed global _rate_limit_exhausted flag and skip-ahead logic
  (no longer needed since we wait for reset)

Newsletter email system:
- New email_service.py with Resend API integration (free 3k/month)
- HTML email templates for verification and weekly newsletter
- Newsletter template includes trending skills, stats, CTA
- Admin endpoint POST /api/admin/newsletter/send
- Admin endpoint POST /api/admin/newsletter/test-verification
- Test mode: send to specific email for testing
- Production mode: send to all verified subscribers
- Falls back to BillionMail if Resend not configured

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove rate limiting, fix step 1 crash

- Remove all Redis/Upstash code — Redis.fromEnv() was crashing at module
  load time because UPSTASH env vars weren't set, causing every /api/analyze
  request to fail with 500 (root cause of step 1 failures)
- Remove rate limiting entirely — unlimited analyses for all users
- Simplified to clean OpenRouter proxy: uses userApiKey if provided, else
  falls back to OPENROUTER_API_KEY env var

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

iPhone 18,2 iOS 26.3 (23D127) firmware components

Key components from IPSW: kernelcache, iBoot, SEP, DCP, ANE, AVE,
AGX, ExclaveCore, ISP, AOP, NFC, and other coprocessor firmware.
DMGs, baseband, plists, and mtree manifests excluded.

fix: prevent OOM crashes by reducing Chrome memory footprint

Chrome instances were leaking memory over time, causing the kernel
OOM-killer to terminate all processes including SSH. This adds aggressive
Chrome flags to reduce per-instance usage, periodic Chrome restarts
every 30 refreshes to free leaked memory, and a 1GB Docker memory limit
to protect the host VM from becoming unresponsive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Kafka-to-TimescaleDB consumer pipeline (#3)

* Add KafkaMessageSerializer.Deserialize + round-trip tests

Add Deserialize method that reverses the envelope format: reads eventType
to determine concrete type, merges base fields + nested data into flat JSON,
then deserializes to the correct TelemetryEvent subtype. Four new test cases
cover CarTelemetry, LapData, CarStatus (with nulls), and unknown event type.

Also adds Git Workflow section to CLAUDE.md with subagent/atomic commit
preferences.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add ITimescaleWriter and TimescaleWriter with Npgsql COPY binary protocol

TimescaleWriter groups events by concrete type and writes each batch to the
corresponding hypertable using Npgsql's COPY binary import for maximum
throughput. Handles nullable fields for CarStatusEvent (partial writes from
CarDamage vs CarStatus packets). Column order matches init.sql exactly.

Adds Npgsql NuGet package. Removes Storage/.gitkeep placeholder.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add KafkaConsumerService background service

Subscribes to car-telemetry, lap-data, and car-status Kafka topics.
Implements dual-trigger batching (size threshold OR time threshold) with
manual offset commits only after successful TimescaleDB writes. Gracefully
flushes remaining buffer on shutdown.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Wire up TimescaleWriter and KafkaConsumerService in DI

Register ITimescaleWriter as singleton and KafkaConsumerService as a hosted
service so both sides of the pipeline (UDP→Kafka and Kafka→TimescaleDB)
run in the same process.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add TimescaleWriter integration tests with Testcontainers

Spins up timescale/timescaledb:latest-pg16 container, runs init.sql to
create hypertables, then tests WriteBatchAsync for each event type:
car telemetry, lap data, car status with nulls, mixed batch, and empty
batch edge case. Verifies row counts and field values via SQL queries.

Adds Npgsql and Testcontainers.PostgreSql packages to test project.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use batch offset commit in KafkaConsumerService

Track highest offset per topic-partition in a dictionary and commit all
offsets in a single Commit() call after successful batch write, instead
of committing per-message in a loop. Reduces Kafka round trips from N
to 1 per flush.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(nd-1md): Implement proactive work stealing for idle workers

Enhanced work stealing system with proactive capabilities:

**New Features:**
- Load-aware work stealing: Idle workers can take beads from overloaded workers
- Queue depth tracking: Heartbeats now include queue_depth field
- Configurable thresholds: Control stealing behavior via config

**Configuration Options:**
- proactive_stealing_enabled: Enable/disable proactive stealing (default: true)
- stealing_load_threshold: Min claimed beads before "overloaded" (default: 2)
- stealing_idle_threshold: Seconds idle before can steal (default: 60)
- stealing_priority_boost: Priority multiplier for stolen beads (default: 1)
- steal_from_active_workers: Allow stealing from workers with heartbeats (default: false)

**New Functions:**
- _needle_proactive_stealing_enabled: Check if proactive stealing is enabled
- _needle_get_stealing_load_threshold: Get overload threshold
- _needle_get_stealing_idle_threshold: Get idle time threshold
- _needle_can_steal_from_active: Check if can steal from active workers
- _needle_get_worker_queue_depth: Get beads claimed by a worker
- _needle_is_worker_overloaded: Check if worker is overloaded
- _needle_get_overloaded_workers: Find all overloaded workers
- _needle_find_stealable_from_overloaded: Find stealable beads from overloaded
- _needle_get_stealing_priority_boost: Get priority boost for stolen beads
- _needle_mark_bead_stolen: Mark bead as stolen for tracking

**Heartbeat Enhancement:**
- Added queue_depth field to track worker load

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Worker <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Move test_notifications_handler to runtime package and remove manual tags (#788)

* test(qemu): remove manual tags from QEMU integration tests

qemu-system-x86 is explicitly installed in the RBE worker image for these
tests. All kernel modules (nftables, WireGuard) are bundled in the initramfs
so no host kernel dependencies are needed. TCG software emulation is used so
no KVM hardware acceleration is required. Docker is available on RBE workers
for the discovery service.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CRtVsTvHubtBFTmZu8kZLo

* test: remove manual tags from mcp_starter integration test and agent_core ollama reasoning mock

mcp_starter:integration_test starts its own MCP server via stdio transport —
no external dependencies needed. Verified it passes in CI via RBE.

agent_core:test_ollama_reasoning uses live_openai_py_test which generates both
.mock and .live targets. The .mock target exercises reasoning item handling via
EchoMock (no API key needed) and passes in CI. The .live target still requires
an Ollama endpoint and OPENAI_API_KEY.

* test(agent_server): move notifications_handler test from e2e/ to runtime/ as Docker test

The test only uses Docker (build_container + policy evaluation), not Playwright.
It was wrongly placed in e2e/ and marked manual. Since RBE has Docker
(test.init-dockerd), it can run in CI.

- Move test to agent_server/runtime/test_notifications_handler.py
- Add runtime_image fixture param (needed to pre-load adgn-runtime:latest
  for self_check policy evaluation via ContainerPolicyEvaluator)
- Use requires_docker=True + size=medium instead of manual tag
- Add data deps for agent_server:load and debian_slim:load

https://claude.ai/code/session_01CRtVsTvHubtBFTmZu8kZLo

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Phase 1-5 improvements — thresholds, per-asset ML, dynamic Kelly, window wicks

Config (local.yaml):
- hard_stop_roi: -0.045 → -0.10 (give trades room past initial noise)
- max_window_progress: 0.85 → 0.65 (avoid late entries with no edge)
- time_stop_seconds_to_expiry_15m: 90 (NEW, 15m only); 1h keeps 300s
- max_spread_bps_15m: 500 → 150; max_spread_bps_1h: 700 → 200 (profitable after fees)
- min_depth_usdc: 0 → 300 (protect against illiquid markets)

Strategy (v3_strategy.rs):
- ML confidence: ×(0.5+edge) → ×(0.7+edge) — lowers bar at low edge
- Fallback score threshold: 2.0 → 1.5 — 2-of-3 indicators sufficient
- Per-asset ML: asset_predictors: HashMap<Asset,MLPredictor> trained separately
  when each asset has >= min_samples; preferred over shared predictor

ML features (ml_engine/features.rs + features/mod.rs):
- Remove rsi_divergence from to_vec() (always 0.0, adds noise)
- Add window_upper_shadow + window_lower_shadow (wick pressure features)
- NUM_FEATURES: 57 → 58

Risk (paper_trading.rs + config/mod.rs + main.rs):
- Per-timeframe time_stop: 15m=90s, 1h=300s (separate config fields)
- Dynamic Kelly uncertainty: sigma = max(0.05, 0.50 - win_rate) after 10+ trades

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
